page: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This server includes 1890 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It does not feature any GPU accelerators. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix operations.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in specific compute tasks. Single-threaded gzip compression at 35.44 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.75 GB/sec rank in the top tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves a strong score of 202.24 GB/sec, while the PassMark CPU score is 48689.02. For language model workloads, the server achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 346.08 tokens/sec on small models, though performance scales down to 17.34 tokens/sec for prompt processing on large models.

The D32lds_v7 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, given its 2.0 GB per vCPU allocation. However, memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching may be constrained by the 64.0 GB RAM limit. The server is suited for compute-bound tasks, web serving, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is an x86_64 server configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, representing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. The processor supports AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and static web serving throughput. It also shows strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium language models. Given the constrained memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is optimized for compute-heavy workloads rather than memory-intensive applications, making it suitable for web serving, text processing, and lightweight inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is an x86_64 server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, delivering strong compute and web serving performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 features 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused server featuring thirty-two dedicated vCPUs, high-speed Intel processors, and local storage for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression benchmark performance
Hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and AMX instruction support
Suited for web serving, text processing, and lightweight model inference
